body:
## Plan ready — BIN-78 Inline Mention Chips

Implementation plan written and posted to the issue description.

**Summary:**
- 4 new files: `MentionEditor` (contenteditable rich text), `MentionMenu` (floating @-triggered dropdown), `MentionChip` (inline chip renderer), `MentionQuickPeek` (hover/tap popover)
- CSS modules for each component
- 2 modified files: `page.tsx` (replace textarea with MentionEditor, update sidebar to scene mentions list), `page.module.css`
- Mention storage format: `[[@Name|type|entity-id]]` encoded in plain text — survives renames/deletes gracefully
- Plain-text export: strip mention encoding to readable labels
- No external rich text library — custom contenteditable with React

Assign to: Code Monkey

## UX goal
Keep writers in drafting flow while making world context instantly available inline. Mentions must feel as fast as typing, low-noise in long prose, and trustworthy as durable links.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: draft text surface (scene writing remains dominant).
2. Secondary: inline mention affordances (chips + lightweight quick peek).
3. Tertiary: mention inventory for current scene in the existing right sidebar.
4. Utility: fallback labels and status messaging (deleted entity, unresolved link).

## Layout and components
- Editor mode (`/story/[id]`): keep current two-column layout in [src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx] with editor main + right sidebar.
- Main editor area: replace plain textarea behavior with rich inline text rendering so mention chips can exist inline (structural change required from current textarea-only surface).
- Mention insertion:
  - Trigger on `@` at caret; open compact command menu anchored near caret.
  - Results grouped by entity type: Character, Location, Rule.
  - Row content: entity name (primary), short descriptor (secondary), type badge.
- Mention chip style in prose:
  - Inline pill with subtle background tint (`--color-polar-100`) and text `--color-carbon-600`.
  - Hover/focus adds border emphasis (`--color-fashion-300` to `--color-elephant-500`) without heavy fill.
- Quick peek card:
  - Opens on hover (desktop) and tap/click (mobile).
  - Compact card with entity title, type, 2-3 key fields, and “Open full details” action.
  - Position as popover near chip; avoid covering active line whenever possible.
- Sidebar updates:
  - Convert current Notes placeholder into “Scene mentions” list for active scene, grouped by type with counts.

## Interaction states
- Idle: editor shows normal prose + chips, no overlays.
- Loading: mention menu shows skeleton rows when fetching entities; quick peek shows short skeleton block.
- Empty:
  - No search matches: “No entities found. Continue typing to keep plain text.”
  - No scene mentions: sidebar empty state with brief hint.
- Error:
  - Mention fetch failure: inline non-blocking message in menu, retry action.
  - Broken/deleted entity link: chip becomes muted + “Missing entity” label in quick peek.
- Success:
  - Insertion confirms by rendering chip immediately at caret.
  - Rename updates propagate to chips/scene list on refresh without text corruption.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard:
  - `@` opens menu, arrow keys navigate, Enter inserts, Esc closes.
  - Chips tabbable in reading order; Enter/Space opens quick peek.
- Focus:
  - Reuse current focus ring pattern (`2px` outline with `--color-carbon-600`).
  - Popover/menu must trap focus only when explicitly opened via keyboard.
- Contrast:
  - Maintain AA contrast using existing grayscale tokens; avoid low-contrast chip text on fog backgrounds.
- Text clarity:
  - Keep chip labels full readable names (no cryptic IDs).
  - Avoid all-caps for dense content except small type labels.

## Visual style
- Match current monochrome design language in `src/styles/tokens.css`.
- Use grayscale tokens only:
  - Surfaces: `--color-white`, `--color-polar-100`
  - Borders/dividers: `--color-fashion-300`
  - Primary text: `--color-black-700` / `--color-carbon-600`
  - Secondary text: `--color-elephant-500`, `--color-cement-400`
- Keep chips understated so prose remains dominant; no saturated accent colors.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop/tablet:
  - Hover quick peek enabled.
  - Sidebar remains visible at >=1024px and carries scene mention inventory.
- Mobile (<1024 currently hides sidebar):
  - Mentions list moves to collapsible panel inside editor header area or bottom sheet.
  - Quick peek opens on tap; dismiss via close button or outside tap.
  - Mention menu should anchor near caret when possible, fallback to bottom sheet on narrow widths.

## Codebase alignment
- Reviewed current structure and primitives:
  - `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`
  - `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`
  - `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx`
  - `src/components/Header/Header.tsx`
  - `src/components/ContentContainer/ContentContainer.tsx`
  - `src/styles/tokens.css`
- Brief is aligned with existing AppShell + Story page layout and tokenized CSS module approach.
- Explicit required structural change: current textarea cannot render inline chips/popovers; editor surface must support inline nodes.

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse:
  - Existing page shell/header/sidebar framing in story editor.
  - Existing token system and focus/interactive style patterns.
  - Existing sidebar section/title visual treatment.
- New build:
  - Mention menu component (caret-anchored).
  - Inline mention chip renderer.
  - Entity quick peek popover/card.
  - Scene mention inventory module in sidebar/mobile equivalent.

## Feasibility notes
- Data model must preserve mention token identity separate from display text to survive renames/deletes.
- Export pipeline must degrade mentions to plain labels deterministically.
- Deleted entities should not break prose selection/editability.
- Popover positioning must handle long documents and scroll containers in editor canvas.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Anchor to current editor route: `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` (editor mode block).
- Preserve current mode switch behavior (`ViewModeSwitch`) and avoid adding multi-click flows for common insertion.
- Keep one-click (or single keystroke) mention insertion path from typing flow.
- Respect existing responsive breakpoint behavior in `page.module.css` where sidebar is hidden below desktop.
- Keep styling in CSS modules and grayscale tokens from `src/styles/tokens.css`.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This feature strengthens a core wedge: continuity-safe drafting without forcing writers out of flow. We are not just adding worldbuilding; we are embedding it directly in prose composition, which creates a faster and less fragmented writing experience than module-hopping workflows.

## Competitor evidence
- Campfire positions world context through dedicated modules and tagged sidebar notes rather than a primary inline mention workflow in the manuscript editor ("Encylopedia module" + "notes sidebar" patterns): https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- LivingWriter emphasizes structure tools (plot board, story organization) and separate story elements management in its public materials; I did not find a clearly marketed inline entity-chip + quick-peek drafting loop in these references: https://guides.livingwriter.com/product-updates/new-plot-board and https://livingwriter.com/blog/story-elements-the-complete-guide-for-fiction-writers/
- Inkitt’s writer-facing proposition is centered on audience growth/reader ecosystem, not deep in-editor worldbuilding linkage, which leaves room for us to differentiate on drafting continuity UX: https://writers.inkitt.com/

## Risks / trade-offs
- Chip-heavy text can add visual noise and reduce reading flow if styling is too prominent.
- Broken or renamed entities can create trust issues unless link behavior is predictable.
- Export/import compatibility can regress if mention fallback rules are not strict.

## Recommendation
Approve with design constraints that preserve writing flow: subtle chip styling, keyboard-first insertion, non-blocking hover peek, and deterministic fallback text behavior on delete/export.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

## Review Request
Requesting Market Research validation on differentiation claim and v1 scope boundaries.

- Focus: inline world context without leaving drafting flow
- Ask: approve/reframe against Campfire/Living Writer workflows and note key risks

Assign to: Market Research
